acmpca.certificate.subject.CommonName.Docs: For CA and end-entity certificates in a private PKI, the common name (CN) can be any string within the length limit.  Note: In publicly trusted certificates, the common name must be a fully qualified domain name (FQDN) associated with the certificate subject.

acmpca.certificate.subject.Country.Docs: Two-digit code that specifies the country in which the certificate subject located.

acmpca.certificate.subject.Distinguished.Docs: Disambiguating information for the certificate subject.

acmpca.certificate.subject.GenerationQua.Docs: Typically a qualifier appended to the name of an individual. Examples include Jr. for junior, Sr. for senior, and III for third.

acmpca.certificate.subject.Initials.Docs: Concatenation that typically contains the first letter of the **GivenName**, the first letter of the middle name if one exists, and the first letter of the **Surname**.

acmpca.certificate.subject.Locality.Docs: The locality (such as a city or town) in which the certificate subject is located.

acmpca.certificate.subject.Organization.Docs: Legal name of the organization with which the certificate subject is affiliated.

acmpca.certificate.subject.Organizationa.Docs: A subdivision or unit of the organization (such as sales or finance) with which the certificate subject is affiliated.

acmpca.certificate.subject.Pseudonym.Docs: Typically a shortened version of a longer **GivenName**. For example, Jonathan is often shortened to John. Elizabeth is often shortened to Beth, Liz, or Eliza.

acmpca.certificate.subject.State.Docs: State in which the subject of the certificate is located.

acmpca.certificate.subject.Surname.Docs: Family name. In the US and the UK, for example, the surname of an individual is ordered last. In Asian cultures the surname is typically ordered first.

acmpca.certificate.subject.Title.Docs: A title such as Mr. or Ms., which is pre-pended to the name to refer formally to the certificate subject.
